title: Thalassa Tarkin
content:
{{Top|fga|leg}} {{Otheruses|Thalassa|more=Tarkin (disambiguation)}} {{Character |type=Galactic Empire |image=[[File:Lady_tarkin.jpg]] |name=Thalassa Tarkin |homeworld= |birth= |death= |species=[[Human/Legends|Human]]"[[Princess Leia, Imperial Servant]]" |gender=[[Gender/Legends|Female]] |pronouns=She/her |height= |mass= |hair=[[Color/Legends|Brown]] |feathers= |eyes= |skin=Light |cyber= |families=new |parents=new |partners=new |siblings=new |children=new |affiliation=[[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] |masters= |apprentices= }} {{Quote|My husband was murdered by Rebels!… I'd kill them myself… if I had the chance!|Thalassa Tarkin|}} The wife of [[Grand Moff/Legends|Grand Moff]] [[Wilhuff Tarkin/Legends|Wilhuff Tarkin]], [[Lady/Legends|Lady]] '''Thalassa Tarkin''' was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|female]] of the influential [[Motti family]] of [[Phelarion/Legends|Phelarion]]. Wilhuff Tarkin [[Marriage/Legends|married]] Thalassa for power, not for love—he had an affair with [[Natasi Daala]] while he was still married. Thalassa Tarkin, however, remained supportive of her husband and the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Empire]], even after his [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|death]] aboard the [[DS-1 Orbital Battle Station/Legends|Death Star]]. She then headed a [[Megonite moss/Legends|megonite moss]] operation on Phelarion, hoping to sell the megonite as ammunition for the Empire so that they might further work to crush the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebellion]]. Just prior to the [[Thirteenth Imperial Diplomatic Conclave]], for which Lady Tarkin was the host, [[Princess/Legends|Princess]] [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa]] infiltrated Tarkin's [[Slavery/Legends|slave]] operation and derailed the transaction of the megonite going to the Empire. [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]], who was on Phelarion for the conclave, held Lady Tarkin personally responsible for the mishap. ==Biography== ===Marriage and family=== A member of the [[Motti family]] on [[Phelarion/Legends|Phelarion]], the wealthy [[Lady/Legends|Lady]] Thalassa Tarkin was [[Marriage/Legends|married]] to [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Governor/Legends|Governor]] [[Wilhuff Tarkin/Legends|Wilhuff Tarkin]]. Wilhuff Tarkin married [[Gender/Legends|her]] due to her powerful family connections,''[[The New Essential Guide to Characters]]'' such as her cousin, [[Commander/Legends|Commander]] [[Conan Antonio Motti/Legends|Conan Motti]].''[[Geonosis and the Outer Rim Worlds]]'' In actuality, Wilhuff Tarkin did not love her; soon after their marriage, he instead began an affair with a young [[Cadet/Legends|cadet]] on [[Carida/Legends|Carida]], [[Natasi Daala]]. Despite the Governor's misgivings for his wife, the couple had one son named [[Garoche Tarkin|Garoche]], who served as an [[Admiral/Legends|admiral]] in the [[Imperial Navy/Legends|Imperial Navy]]. In [[19 BBY/Legends|19 BBY]], Garoche secretly defected to the [[Atoan system]] and aided in its insurgency against the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]]. The official reports read that he had gone missing and was later [[Death/Legends|killed]] by [[Atoan]] natives. However, the real murderer was secretly [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine's]] [[Supreme Commander (Galactic Empire)/Legends|right hand man]], [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]], who along with his [[Sith Master/Legends|master]] believed that Garoche's death would turn Governor Tarkin into a powerful, hate-driven ally that would show the Empire's enemies no mercy. Their plan succeeded.''[[Star Wars: Darth Vader and the Lost Command]]'' On Governor Tarkin's [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] of [[Eriadu/Legends|Eriadu]] in the post-[[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]] era, the [[Tarkin family/Legends|Tarkin family]] had earned a reputation as a force to be reckoned with. When members of the [[Nebula Front/Legends|Nebula Front]] kidnapped Lady Tarkin's niece [[Shella Motti]], the Tarkins hired agents to find the missing girl; Motti, however, hired their own agents to prevent her from having to go home to her family. In [[5 BBY/Legends|5 BBY]], when Lady Tarkin's brother-in-law [[Gideon Tarkin]] was killed during the [[Erhynradd Mutiny]], his daughter [[Rivoche Tarkin|Rivoche]] was invited to come live at the Tarkin estate; however, the teenage Rivoche frequently acted out in resentment, so Governor Tarkin had her sent to an exclusive preparatory academy on [[Clær]].''[[Cracken's Rebel Operatives]]'' Around [[1 BBY/Legends|1 BBY]], Thalassa Tarkin and her spouse lived in an [[Wilhuff Tarkin's apartment|apartment]] on [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]]. In that apartment, Thalassa learned of the existence of the [[Death Star/Legends|Death Star]], a [[Moon/Legends|moon]]-sized [[Battle station/Legends|battle station]] built by the Empire. Her husband explained to her that he had been given an new permanent assignment on the Death Star and showed her a [[Hologram/Legends|hologram]] of that [[Superweapon/Legends|superweapon]].{{Topps|set=2013 Topps Star Wars Illustrated: A New Hope|cardname=One Year Earlier: Grand Moff Tarkin|link=https://www.tcdb.com/ViewCard.cfm/sid/87474/cid/6296783/2013-Topps-Star-Wars-Illustrated-A-New-Hope---One-Year-Earlier-OY-11-Grand-Moff-Tarkin}} ===Widow=== While [[Grand Moff/Legends|Grand Moff]] Tarkin was killed aboard the [[DS-1 Orbital Battle Station/Legends|Death Star]] when it was [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|destroyed]] by the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]], Imperial news agencies reported that he had died in a [[Shuttle/Legends|shuttle]] accident at the [[Tallaani Shipyards|Tallaan shipyards]]. Lady Tarkin, however, knew the truth; she became obsessed with avenging his death by crushing the Rebellion. At [[Port Tarkin]] on Phelarion, she had a solid black monument constructed in his honor. The plaque on it read:
IN MEMORY OF MY HUSBAND, WE SERVE THE EMPIRE FAITHFULLY!
====Megonite mogul==== Lady Tarkin was the heiress of a mining industry for [[Megonite moss/Legends|megonite moss]] on Phelarion, started by [[Lithio Motti]] centuries prior.{{Hyperspace|url=hyperspace/source/praji/20090205.html|text=Tinker, Tailor, Soldier, Praji|int=Tinker, Tailor, Soldier, Praji}} Tarkin ran the business as an Imperial labor colony and used [[Slavery/Legends|slaves]] to [[Mining/Legends|mine]] the explosive, heat-sensitive moss to develop ammunition for the Empire, to which she remained fanatically devoted, particularly for their dedication to destroying the Rebellion. Just prior to the [[Thirteenth Imperial Diplomatic Conclave]], which Lady Tarkin was hosting, [[Princess/Legends|Princess]] [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa]] became stranded on Phelarion. Mistaking her for a slave girl, Tarkin chose Organa, whom she called "Lerna," as a servant to work at the conclave. After notifying an Imperial [[Freighter/Legends|freighter]] to postpone its megonite pickup due to an explosion at the mine, Tarkin discovered that a large amount of megonite was not being accounted for and began to suspect thievery. Tarkin was then contacted by [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]] about the delay in landing, and he accused her of selling the megonite to the Rebel Alliance. Incensed, Tarkin set out to find whomever was stealing her megonite. [[File:13thConclave.jpg|thumb|left|250px|Lady Thalassa Tarkin welcomes the guests at the Thirteenth Imperial Diplomatic Conclave.]] Tarkin returned to her private chambers after hearing a loud noise; there she found her servant [[Nardo]] dead from an explosion in her megonite display case. She nevertheless proceeded to her banquet hall to host the Imperial Diplomatic Conclave, for guests such as [[Shea Hublin]], Chancellor [[Qua]] of the [[Zenox Star Cluster]], the [[Most High Proctor]] of [[Thebeon 8]], and Darth Vader himself. Tarkin had an embarrassing moment when she offered a [[Orgone bubbler/Legends|drink]] to Vader, who reminded her that he neither ate nor drank. Vader grew impatient with Tarkin and again accused her of selling to the Rebels. His [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtroopers]] then discovered the crashed [[Gossamer glider|Rebel glider]] Organa had used for landing. When Tarkin spotted her servant girl Lerna and two other slaves fleeing the scene, Vader recognized the girl as Princess Leia Organa. While Tarkin doubted the Rebels' chances of escaping the [[Planet/Legends|planet]], Vader vowed to hold Tarkin personally responsible if Organa escaped. Ultimately, the Princess was able to make it offworld.''[[The Essential Guide to Characters]]'' ==Personality and traits== Thalassa Tarkin was a severe{{DB|character|grandmofftarkin|Tarkin, Grand Moff}} and stately woman who idolized her husband. Even though Wilhuff Tarkin's heart belonged to another woman, Lady Tarkin considered the man to be a martyr and a hero, and remained faithful to his cause. She took pride in her devotion to the Empire, and was highly offended when Darth Vader accused her of selling war matériel to the Rebellion. Tarkin was also paranoid, fearing that Rebels were everywhere, trying to kill her. Despite her paranoid nature, she never once suspected that her handmaiden Lehra was not only a Rebel agent, but in fact Princess Leia. ==Behind the scenes== {{Quote|Lady Tarkin's first name, '''Thalassa''', is new. I get the feeling this will be what's remembered in five or ten years, after the rest of the article has been left by the wayside of continuity. Still, it's not a bad mark to have made.|Nathan O'Keefe, in the endnotes for "Tinker, Tailor, Soldier, Praji"|{{Blog|valinkenobi|15|TTSP Endnotes: Volume 2}}}} Lady Tarkin first appeared in [[Russ Manning]]'s [[1979]] [[Star Wars (comic strip)|newspaper strip]] ''[[Princess Leia, Imperial Servant]]''. Thirty years after her debut, she was given the first name of "Thalassa" by [[Nathan O'Keefe]] in his [[2009]] short story "[[Tinker, Tailor, Soldier, Praji]]." Thalassa means "Sea" in Greek and was the name of a primordial sea goddess in Greek myth,{{WebCite|url=http://www.pantheon.org/articles/t/thalassa.html|text=Source for the Goddess name|archivedate=20010625144401}} as well as a moon of Neptune.{{WP|Thalassa (moon)|Thalassa (moon)}} O'Keefe chose the name due to it sounding "cool and [[Star Wars|Star-Warsy]]," rather than for any thematic significance of its existing meanings. ==Appearances== *"[[Princess Leia, Imperial Servant]]" {{Reprint|Princess Leia, Imperial Servant}} {{1st}} ==Sources== {{Mediacat|imagecat=Images of Thalassa Tarkin}} *''[[The Essential Guide to Characters]]'' *{{GalaxyCite|11|ComicScan|ComicScan: Paint Him Black}} {{Imo}} *{{CollectorCite|3|ComicScan|ComicScan: Coming Full Circle}}
